headline: Investors aren't impressed: Robinhood's publicly traded VC fund down 15.8% in NYSE debut
summary: Shares of Robinhood‘s first closed-end VC fund for retail investors ended their first day trading on the New York Stock Exchange down 15.8% from their IPO price of $25. The vehicle marks the latest effort by Wall Street to offer private-market products directly to retail investors. Yet its disappointing first day of trading—at a discount to the fund’s NAV—may suggest investors aren’t as impressed by its holdings. “That discount to NAV will be the proxy for how overvalued venture capital is,” Ram

headline: Aetherflux Appoints Matthew Seaford to Advisory Board
summary: SAN CARLOS, Calif., March 06, 2026--Aetherflux, the orbital energy infrastructure company founded by Robinhood co-founder Baiju Bhatt, today announced the appointment of Matthew Seaford to its Advisory Board. Seaford joins Aetherflux as the company scales its mission to build the first American power grid in space, optimizing energy delivery for both terrestrial needs and orbital AI compute.

headline: Robinhood CEO talks new private markets fund, Platinum credit card
summary: Brokerage platform Robinhood Markets (HOOD) is launching its own private markets retail fund, which has already raised $650 million. Robinhood chairman and CEO Vlad Tenev speaks with Yahoo Finance Executive Editor Brian Sozzi about the company's new private markets fund, the release of its new Platinum credit card, and the outlook for the IPO market in 2026. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination.

headline: Week’s Best: Banks’ and Brokerage Firms’ Very Bad Day
summary: Fridays have been rough trading days for bank, brokerage, and wealth management stocks lately.  Last week’s declines were linked to investor worries about disruption from artificial intelligence and troubles in private credit.  The declines today were more about the impact of war in Iran, which is likely to lead to inflation as oil prices spike, and a terrible U.S. jobs report.

headline: Robinhood’s Venture Fund Begins Trading. What to Know.
summary: Robinhood  wants to make private investments more accessible to retail investors, and on Friday the company moved closer to that goal through the launch of its first fund, a publicly traded venture capital fund that invests in privately held companies.  Robinhood Ventures Fund  I, which is structured as a closed-end fund, began trading on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol RVI.  Robinhood said in a blog post last month that it has agreed to buy shares of payments company Stripe, but wouldn’t be able to until after the fund’s launch.

Robinhood Ventures Fund  I, which is structured as a closed-end fund, began trading on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol RVI.  Robinhood said in a blog post last month that it has agreed to buy shares of payments company Stripe, but wouldn’t be able to until after the fund’s launch.

headline: Robinhood Launches Fund Offering Private-Market Investing
summary: Robinhood’s first fund offering private-market access to individual investors is officially on the market.  The Robinhood Ventures Fund I began trading on the New York Stock Exchange on Friday, at a price of $25 a share.  The fund is a publicly traded venture capital vehicle that allows traders to invest in a group of private companies like software company Databricks, fintech firm Revolut and health-tracking ringmaker Oura.

headline: Investors Dump Bank, Brokerage Stocks As Oil Spikes and Jobs Report Disappoints
summary: Financial stocks can’t catch a break.  Investors dumped shares of banks, brokerages, and other financial services companies on Friday morning after the U.S. economy was shown to be shedding jobs and the war with Iran roiled oil markets.  The KBW Nasdaq Bank Index fell as much as 4.5% at the open before paring some of its losses; the index was down 3.7% at 10 a.m. The  which tracks a basket of financial services companies, was down 2.4%.

headline: Robinhood's $658 million private markets fund for retail investors goes public
summary: Robinhood debuted its flagship $658.4 million venture fund on the New York Stock Exchange on Friday, offering retail investors ‌a rare chance to invest in high-profile, privately held technology companies. Private company ‌investments have long been the playground of Silicon Valley's biggest venture capital firms, with retail investors largely ​shut out of the booming market where valuations have surged in recent years. The fund, which began trading under the ticker 'RVI', has investments in prominent private companies, including software startup Databricks, fintech Ramp and financial services firm Revolut.

The fund, which began trading under the ticker 'RVI', has investments in prominent private companies, including software startup Databricks, fintech Ramp and financial services firm Revolut.

headline: Robinhood’s Venture Fund to Begin Trading. What to Know.
summary: Robinhood  wants to make private investments more accessible to retail investors, and on Friday the company may move closer to that goal through the launch of its first fund, a publicly traded venture capital fund that invests in privately held companies.  Robinhood Ventures Fund I, which is structured as a closed-end fund, is expected to begin trading on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol RVI.  The fund is set to offer exposure to a range of private companies including fintech Airwallex, aerospace company Boom Technology, and data and artificial intelligence company Databricks.

Robinhood Ventures Fund I, which is structured as a closed-end fund, is expected to begin trading on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol RVI.  The fund is set to offer exposure to a range of private companies including fintech Airwallex, aerospace company Boom Technology, and data and artificial intelligence company Databricks.

headline: Robinhood Tests New Premium Services As Volatile Stock Eyes Deeper Ties
summary: Robinhood Markets (NasdaqGS:HOOD) has rolled out a $695 Platinum credit card, a new Family Hub with custodial and trust accounts, and an Early Dividends program. The moves mark a push beyond trading into premium, full-service finance aimed at higher income and multi account households. This combined launch signals an effort to compete more directly with established card issuers and large banks offering bundled wealth and banking services.
